sections.paragraph:
μωμοσκοπέομαι (μῶμος, σκοπέω) mid. dep.; 1 aor. pass. ptc. μωμοσκοπηθείς (Const. Apost. 2, 3; the noun μωμοσκόπος in Philo, Agr. 130; Clem. Al., Strom. 4, 18, 117) examine for blemishes.

ⓐ t.t. in sacrificial usage: of a sacrificial animal, etc. 1 Cl 41:2.

ⓑ fig. of God, who πάντα μωμοσκοπεῖται examines everything (for blemishes) Pol 4:3.—Cp. DELG s.v. σκέπτομαι.

dep. dep. = deponent

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

Clem. Al. Clem. Al. = Clement of Alexandria, II–III a.d.—List 5

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 663
